A1-M01.title: Subject Pronouns & The Verb 'To Be' (Affirmative)
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede presentarse a sí mismo y a otros, pedir y dar información personal básica.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Introduce yourself using subject pronouns and the verb to be
can_do_statements: I can introduce myself.
I can say where I am from.
I can say my profession or how I feel.
A1-M01.theory: In English, every sentence needs a subject. The subject pronouns are: I, You, He, She, It, We, They. The verb 'to be' changes form: I am, You are, He/She/It is, We/They are.

common_mistakes.explanation: The pronoun 'I' always pairs with 'am', never with 'is'.

common_mistakes.correction: He is happy. / She is happy.
common_mistakes.explanation: In English, you cannot drop the subject pronoun. You must say who is happy.

vocabulary.question: Persona que estudia en una universidad.
Persona que trabaja en un hospital.
Pronombre para hablar de uno mismo.

reading.text: Hello! My name is Carlos. I am a student. I am from Mexico City. I am happy today. My friend is a doctor. She is from Guadalajara. We are at the campus. They are my classmates. He is an engineer. It is a good day.

A1-M01.title: Negatives & Yes/No Questions with To Be
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede hacer preguntas simples y dar respuestas negativas sobre información personal.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Form negatives and yes/no questions with the verb to be
can_do_statements: I can say what I am not.
I can ask if someone is a student.
I can answer yes or no questions.
A1-M01.theory: To make negatives with 'to be', add 'not' after the verb: I am not, He is not (He isn't), They are not (They aren't). To make Yes/No questions, invert the subject and verb: Are you a student? Is she happy?

common_mistakes.explanation: In English, 'not' goes AFTER the verb 'to be', not before it.

common_mistakes.correction: Are you not a teacher? / Aren't you a teacher?
common_mistakes.explanation: To ask a question, move the verb before the subject.

A1-M01.title: Wh- Questions & Possessive Adjectives
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede hacer y responder preguntas con palabras interrogativas básicas.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Ask and answer Wh- questions and use possessive adjectives
can_do_statements: I can ask where someone is from.
I can ask what someone does.
I can use my, your, his, her.
A1-M01.theory: Wh- questions start with: What (thing), Where (place), Who (person), How (manner/feeling). Structure: Wh-word + verb + subject? Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her, its, our, their.

common_mistakes.explanation: In questions, the verb comes before the subject.

common_mistakes.error: This is my phone of Carlos.
common_mistakes.correction: This is Carlos's phone. / This is his phone.
common_mistakes.explanation: Use possessive 's or possessive adjectives.

A1-M01.title: Plural Nouns & Articles (a/an/the)
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede usar artículos y plurales correctamente en oraciones simples.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Use articles (a/an/the) and form plural nouns correctly
can_do_statements: I can say 'a book' and 'books'.
I can use 'the' for specific things.
I can distinguish between a and an.
A1-M01.theory: Use 'a' before consonant sounds, 'an' before vowel sounds. Use 'the' for specific or known nouns. Plural nouns: add -s (books), -es (boxes), or change -y to -ies (cities).

common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'an' before words that start with a vowel sound.

common_mistakes.correction: I like music. / I like the music in this class.
common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'the' only for specific, known music.

A1-M01.title: Present Simple: Affirmative (Regular Verbs)
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede describir rutinas y hábitos usando el presente simple.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Use the present simple tense for regular verbs
can_do_statements: I can say what I do every day.
I can describe my routine.
I can use he/she + verb-s.
A1-M01.theory: Present Simple describes habits and routines. Structure: I/You/We/They + base verb. He/She/It + verb+s/es. Add -s for most verbs, -es for verbs ending in s/sh/ch/x, change -y to -ies.

common_mistakes.explanation: For he/she/it, add -s or -es to the verb.
For routines, use present simple, not present continuous.

A1-M01.title: Present Simple: Negatives & Questions
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede hacer preguntas y dar negativas sobre rutinas y hábitos.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Form negatives and questions in present simple
can_do_statements: I can say what I don't do.
I can ask about someone's routine.
I can use do/does.
A1-M01.theory: Negatives: I/You/We/They + don't + verb. He/She/It + doesn't + verb. Questions: Do + I/you/we/they + verb? Does + he/she/it + verb?

common_mistakes.explanation: For he/she/it, use 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

A1-M01.title: There is / There are
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede describir lugares y cosas que existen o no existen.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Use there is and there are to describe existence
can_do_statements: I can say 'There is a book on the table.'

A1-M01.theory: Use 'There is' + singular noun. Use 'There are' + plural noun. Questions: Is there...? Are there...? Negatives: There isn't... / There aren't...

common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'there are' with plural nouns.

common_mistakes.error: There are a book on the table.
common_mistakes.correction: There is a book on the table.
common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'there is' with singular nouns.

A1-M01.title: Countable & Uncountable Nouns / Some & Any
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede distinguir entre sustantivos contables e incontables y usar some/any.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Distinguish countable/uncountable nouns and use some/any
can_do_statements: I can say 'some books' and 'some water'.
I can use 'any' in questions and negatives.
I can say 'a lot of' for both.
A1-M01.theory: Countable nouns: can be counted (books, students). Uncountable nouns: cannot be counted (water, rice, music). Use 'some' in affirmatives, 'any' in negatives and questions. 'A lot of' works for both.

common_mistakes.explanation: 'Information' is uncountable in English.
Use 'any' in questions, not 'some'.

A1-M01.title: Prepositions of Place & Time
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede usar preposiciones básicas de lugar y tiempo en oraciones simples.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Use basic prepositions of place and time
can_do_statements: I can say where things are (in, on, at, under, near).
I can say when things happen (at, on, in).
I can give simple directions.
A1-M01.theory: Place: in (enclosed), on (surface), at (point), under (below), near (close to), behind (back of), next to (beside). Time: at (time, night), on (days, dates), in (months, years, morning/afternoon/evening).

common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'in' for parts of the day: in the morning, in the afternoon.

common_mistakes.error: The cat is in the table.

common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'on' for surfaces, 'in' for enclosed spaces.

A1-M01.title: Present Continuous
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede describir acciones que están ocurriendo ahora y planes futuros cercanos.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Talk about actions happening now and near future plans
can_do_statements: I can say what I am doing right now.
I can say what someone else is doing.
I can talk about my plans for this week.
A1-M01.theory: The Present Continuous is formed with: Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing. Use it for actions happening now (I am reading), and for future plans (I am meeting my friend tomorrow). Keywords: now, right now, at the moment, today, this week, tomorrow, next week.

common_mistakes.explanation: Present Continuous needs the auxiliary verb 'am/is/are' before the -ing form.
In Present Continuous, the main verb takes -ing form, not the base form.

A1-M01.title: Modal Verb 'Can'
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede hablar sobre habilidades, pedir permiso y hacer solicitudes.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Talk about abilities, ask for permission, and make requests
can_do_statements: I can talk about what I can and cannot do.
I can ask for permission using 'Can I...?'
I can make polite requests using 'Can you...?'
A1-M01.theory: The modal verb 'can' is used for ability, permission, and requests. Structure: Subject + can + base verb (no 'to'). Negative: can't (cannot). Questions: Can + subject + base verb? Examples: I can swim. Can I open the window? Can you help me?

common_mistakes.explanation: Modal verbs like 'can' are followed directly by the base verb, never 'to + verb'.
In questions with 'can', the base verb follows the subject directly without 'to'.

A1-M01.title: My Family & My Home
A1-M01.mcer_descriptor: Puede describir a su familia y su hogar usando vocabulario y gramática básica.
A1-M01.mcer_goal: Describe your family members and your home using basic grammar
can_do_statements: I can name my family members.
I can describe my house or apartment.
I can say how many rooms and people there are.
A1-M01.theory: Family vocabulary: mother, father, sister, brother, grandparents, aunt, uncle, cousin. House vocabulary: bedroom, living room, kitchen, bathroom, garden, garage. Use 'There is/There are' to describe rooms. Use 'I have' for family. Use adjectives: big, small, old, new, beautiful, comfortable.

common_mistakes.error: I have a brother. His name are Juan.
common_mistakes.correction: I have a brother. His name is Juan.
common_mistakes.explanation: Use 'is' with singular names: His name is Juan.
Use 'there are' with plural nouns: There are three bedrooms.

grammar.question: I ___ a student from this area.

reading.text: I live in a nice neighborhood. There are many stores, parks, and people. My neighbors are friendly. I like my area because it is safe and quiet.

listening.script: A: What do you like about your neighborhood? B: I like the parks and the stores. A: Is it quiet? B: Yes, it is very quiet and safe.

speaking.prompt: Introduce yourself: Say 'I am from this neighborhood.'
Describe your home: Say 'My house is small but comfortable.'

5.title: Daily Routines

5.theory: Use Present Simple for habits. I wake up, She works, They eat. Vocabulary: wake, eat, work, study, sleep.

listening.script: A: What time do you wake up? B: I wake up at 6:30 AM. A: What do you do first? B: I take a shower and eat breakfast. A: What do you eat? B: I eat cereal and drink coffee.

speaking.prompt: Describe your morning: Say 'I wake up at 7 AM every day.'

Describe work/school: Say 'I work from Monday to Friday.'
Talk about lunch: Say 'I eat lunch at noon.'
Describe evening: Say 'I study English at night.'
Talk about sleep: Say 'I sleep at 10 PM.'
